pr.title: Rename `ft` TUs to closer match state names
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Large fighter translation-unit rename pass that changed many `ft` source and asm file names from older full-character, lowercase, or numbered names toward shorter character prefixes plus state/action names. The PR explicitly notes that not all names are accurate and many files remained unsplit, but the new names are closer to fighter state names. Build references in `obj_files.mk`, docs links, and include/reference sites in fighter files were updated to match the renamed TUs.
what_changed: Renamed many fighter C translation units to a short `ftXX_Action` naming scheme, for example `ftMario_Init.c` to `ftMr_Init.c`, `ftfox.c` to `ftFx_Init.c`, `ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c` to `ftMt_SpecialHi.c`, and `ftGameWatch_Attack100.c` to `ftGw_Attack100.c`.

Renamed numbered or vague fighter files to state-number or action-oriented names, for example Donkey Kong files such as `ftdonkey1`/`ftdonkey_walk` became `ftDk_MS_341`/`ftDk_MS_342`, and Master Hand files such as `ftMasterHand_02` became `ftMh_MS_341`.

Renamed corresponding asm stubs with 100% similarity where files were still assembly-backed, such as `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftkirby.s` to `ftKb_Init.s`, `ftlink.s` to `ftLk_Init.s`, and Sheik special move asm files to `ftSk_Special*.s`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` so the build object list points at the renamed C and asm file paths while preserving object ordering.
Updated at least one documentation link in `docs/getting_started.md` from `ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c` to `ftMt_SpecialHi.c`.
Touched many fighter data and support files so source references, headers, and includes followed the renamed TUs.
smart_moves: Used a scripted rename list in the PR body (`rename_tu old new`) to make a very large rename pass auditable and reproducible.
Preserved content identity for many asm renames, shown by `similarity index 100%`, indicating this was primarily a path/name cleanup rather than behavioral code churn.
Kept object file list updates centralized in `obj_files.mk`, which is critical for matching projects because object ordering and source/asm paths affect the build.
Adopted names that combine fighter abbreviation and state/action label, making files easier to correlate with motion states and future split/decomp work.
Explicitly documented that some names were still imperfect, avoiding overclaiming accuracy while still improving the baseline.
decomp_lessons: When renaming decomp translation units, update C files, matching asm stubs, object lists, docs, includes, and any data-reference files in one pass to avoid build drift.
Large rename-only PRs should preserve object order and avoid unrelated logic changes so reviewers and future bisects can treat them as metadata/name cleanup.
For unsplit or poorly understood fighter files, a closer provisional name such as `MS_###` or `Unk#` can be better than a misleading full action name, as long as uncertainty is visible.
State-number-based names are useful for files whose exact action purpose is not yet established but whose motion/state ID relationship is known.
A visible rename script in the PR description is valuable knowledge: it records old-to-new mapping even after GitHub diff truncation or file move collapsing.
naming_conventions: Fighter TUs use `ft` plus a short character code, underscore, and action/category name: examples include `ftCa_Init`, `ftDk_SpecialN`, `ftFx_Appeal`, `ftMt_SpecialHi`, `ftZd_SpecialLw`.
`Init` is used for character initialization/callback/data setup files that formerly had generic lowercase character names such as `ftfox`, `ftpeach`, or `ftpurin`.
`SpecialN`, `SpecialS`, `SpecialHi`, and `SpecialLw` encode neutral, side, up, and down specials in file names.
`Attack100`, `Attack11`, `AttackAir`, `AttackLw3`, `AttackS4`, `AttackHi4`, and similar action names are retained as state/action suffixes.
`MS_###` names are used where a file maps to a motion/state number but the exact semantic action name is not asserted, especially for Master Hand and Donkey Kong files.
`Unk#` names are retained for uncertain files, such as `ftDk_Unk05`, `ftKp_Unk1`, and `ftNn_Unk0`.

Character abbreviations evidenced in this PR include `Ca` Captain, `Cl` Young/Child Link, `Ch` Crazy Hand, `Dk` Donkey Kong, `Dr` Dr. Mario, `Fe` Fire Emblem, `Fc` Falco, `Fx` Fox, `Gw` Game & Watch, `Gn` Ganon, `Gk` Giga Koopa, `Kb` Kirby, `Kp` Koopa/Bowser, `Lk` Link, `Lg` Luigi, `Mr` Mario, `Ms` Marth/Mars, `Mh` Master Hand, `Mt` Mewtwo, `Nn` Nana, `Ns` Ness, `Pe` Peach, `Pc` Pichu, `Pk` Pikachu, `Pp` Popo, `Pr` Purin/Jigglypuff, `Ss` Samus, `Sb` Sandbag, `Sk` Sheik/Seak, `Ys` Yoshi, `Bo` Zako Boy, `Gl` Zako Girl, and `Zd` Zelda.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed asm fallback files alongside C files with no content changes, preserving 100% similarity and keeping mixed C/asm matching targets aligned.
Maintained `obj_files.mk` path updates in the same object-order positions, which helps preserve binary layout and matching behavior.
The PR body's `rename_tu` script indicates the project had or used a helper from `tools/functions.sh` for coordinated TU renames.

Some mappings corrected apparent action-name swaps, such as Captain Falcon `ftCaptain_SpecialLw.s` being renamed to `ftCa_SpecialHi.s` and `ftCaptain_SpecialHi.s` to `ftCa_SpecialLw.s`; evidence is from the diff, but the PR provides no further explanation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR context.

The author stated that not all new names were accurate and many files were still unsplit, so the rename should be treated as an incremental naming improvement rather than final semantic documentation.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that split or semantically renamed `ft*_MS_###` files introduced here.
Check whether Captain Falcon `SpecialHi`/`SpecialLw` swapped rename was later confirmed or corrected.
Search for remaining `ftdonkey*`, `ftMasterHand_##`, or lowercase fighter TU names after PR 781.
Look for project documentation or scripts defining `rename_tu` in `tools/functions.sh`.
Compare later fighter abbreviation conventions against this PR's `ftXX_Action` scheme.
